acceptance_targets: [chapter_02_F01] PASS if the revised exchange at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:72-75 contains no setting in Marisol's rebuttal that was not explicitly introduced by Salvador in the immediately preceding reply; FAIL if any extra setting appears without setup.

[chapter_02_F02] PASS if the revised reveal includes at least one direct, non-euphemistic causal noun tied to Tomas's death context and an immediate verification challenge (date, location, witness, or document) in the same exchange; FAIL if the beat remains only metaphor plus negation.

[chapter_02_F04] PASS if the revised post-meeting section introduces at least one new complication not present in the earlier street-call scene and avoids repeating the same verification standard verbatim; FAIL if it primarily restates earlier guidance.

[FBR-C01-008] Pass if the cited span conveys stress through a different concrete bodily signal and removes throat-tightening language; fail if 'throat tightened/closed' wording (or a near-identical variant) remains.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised exchange at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:72-75 contains no setting in Marisol's rebuttal that was not explicitly introduced by Salvador in the immediately preceding reply; FAIL if any extra setting appears without setup.
findings.problem: The scene introduces an ungrounded factual element ("bed") in Marisol's evidence challenge even though Salvador's prior list names only meetings, church, and studio. That local contradiction weakens the chapter's evidence-driven logic and Marisol's credibility as a precise interviewer.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:74, either (a) revise Marisol's list so it only references settings Salvador already named at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:72, or (b) add a concrete prior mention of "bed" in Salvador's line at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:72 so the challenge remains internally consistent.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised reveal includes at least one direct, non-euphemistic causal noun tied to Tomas's death context and an immediate verification challenge (date, location, witness, or document) in the same exchange; FAIL if the beat remains only metaphor plus negation.
findings.problem: Sanitization drift at the core reveal beat: the death-claim language stays euphemistic or negative (metaphor and 'not' framing) at the exact moment the narrative asks for concrete causal stakes. The chapter withholds naming the suspected mechanism, which softens pressure instead of sharpening it.
findings.rewrite_direction: In the interview reveal span at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:42-60 and/or snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:120-147, add at least one explicit, concrete causal term (for example overdose/drug-use context tied to the foil detail) while preserving uncertainty about date/address/witness. Keep Marisol's verification standard active in the same beat.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised post-meeting section introduces at least one new complication not present in the earlier street-call scene and avoids repeating the same verification standard verbatim; FAIL if it primarily restates earlier guidance.
findings.problem: The chapter resolves the craving beat, then re-runs an almost identical evidence/accountability loop in the post-meeting call. That duplication slows narrative consequence and blunts escalation at the end of the chapter.
findings.rewrite_direction: Condense the second phone exchange at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:362-390 by removing repeated checklist content already established in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:232-285, and replace the recovered space with a new consequence beat (new obstacle, contradiction, or cost) that advances chapter-to-chapter momentum.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the cited span conveys stress through a different concrete bodily signal and removes throat-tightening language; fail if 'throat tightened/closed' wording (or a near-identical variant) remains.
findings.problem: Recurring full-book somatic pattern: 'throat tightened/closed' appears in 4 chapters (chapter_02:646, chapter_07:2063, chapter_08:2526, chapter_12:4278). For pattern control, this chapter's instance should REPLACE.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:646-647, replace the throat-tightening cue with a scene-specific physical stress marker from this AA room context (e.g., cup tremor, breath snag, jaw bite, voice fracture) while preserving the same disclosure beat.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: When choosing between reputationally safe abstraction and specific discomfort, choose specificity. Render addiction, sexuality, deceit, class shame, and grief at lived scale. In edge cases, preserve character truth and causal accountability over reader comfort or institutional decorum.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene demands bodily, moral, or economic specificity, euphemistic phrasing is treated as a failed draft and must be rewritten with concrete stakes and observable consequence.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high - contractions are the spoken default; full forms appear only for emphasis, ritual language, or character-specific formal posture

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate scene density and pressure modes: pair high-conflict chapters with quiet domestic or documentary chapters, vary dialogue-to-interiority ratios, and prevent identical chapter skeletons in consecutive sequence.
prose_style_profile.diction: contemporary literary diction with class-aware, borough-specific concreteness; avoid prestige-neutral workshop language
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third centered on Marisol, with rare brief distance expansions for institutional scenes
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: alternating tensile compression and reflective extension; line-level cadence should carry emotional pressure, not just information
